enonce.texte:
Exercise 1: Future Tenses Identification

Read the following sentences and identify the future tense used: will, going to, or Present Continuous for future. Then, explain why that tense is used.

questions.texte: She is meeting her friend at the mall tomorrow.
I think it will rain this evening.
They are going to build a new school in our neighbourhood.
Look at those clouds! It's going to rain.
I promise I will call you when I arrive.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 2: Transforming Sentences

Rewrite the following sentences using the future tense indicated in brackets. Make any necessary changes.

questions.texte: They have a plan to visit their grandparents next month. (going to)
questions.reponse: They are going to visit their grandparents next month.

questions.texte: Perhaps she will be a doctor one day. (may)
questions.reponse: She may be a doctor one day.

questions.texte: We have arranged to have dinner at 8 pm. (Present Continuous)
questions.reponse: We are having dinner at 8 pm.

questions.texte: I decide to help you now. (will)
The evidence shows that she will win the competition. (going to)
questions.reponse: She is going to win the competition.

intitule: Future Tenses: Choose the Correct Form

enonce.texte:
Exercise 3: Multiple Choice

Choose the correct future form to complete each sentence.

questions.texte: I __________ to the market later. (will go / am going to go / am going)
questions.reponse: am going (Present Continuous for planned action)

questions.texte: The phone is ringing. I __________ answer it. (will / am going to / am)
She __________ her exam because she has studied hard. (will pass / is passing / is going to pass)
The government __________ a new policy next year. (will announce / is announcing / is going to announce)
questions.reponse: is going to announce or is announcing (planned intention)

questions.texte: If you don't hurry, you __________ miss the bus. (will / are going to / are)

enonce.texte:
Exercise 4: Future Tenses in Context

Complete the conversation between two friends, Amina and John, about their plans for the weekend. Use the appropriate future tense: will, going to, or Present Continuous.

questions.texte: Amina: Hi John! What __________ (you / do) this weekend?
questions.reponse: are you doing (Present Continuous - planned action)

questions.texte: John: I __________ (visit) my grandmother in the village. It's already planned.
Amina: That sounds nice! I __________ (help) my father at the farm. We have been talking about it.
John: Oh, I think it __________ (be) a lot of work. But it's good.
Amina: Yes, but I __________ (not / stay) there too long. I have to return for my math class.
John: I __________ (call) you when I get back, okay?
Amina: Great! Have a nice weekend.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 5: Future Plans and Predictions

You are the leader of a student club in your school. Next month, your club will organize a charity event. Write a short paragraph (5-7 sentences) describing:

  • What you have already arranged (Present Continuous)
  • What you intend to do (going to)
  • What you predict for the event (will)

questions.texte: Write your paragraph here.
questions.reponse: I am meeting with the club members this Saturday to plan everything. We are preparing posters and decorations for the event. We are going to collect donations from the community. We are going to set up booths for different activities. I think the event will be very successful. Many people will come to support us. I believe we will raise enough money for the orphanage.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 6: Analysing Future Tenses

Read the following paragraph and identify the future tenses used. Then, explain why the author chose each form.

"Next year, we are planning to build a new community library. The local government will provide some funding, but we are going to organise a fundraiser as well. Look at the support! I think people will donate generously. The architect is coming on Monday to finalise the plans. I am meeting him at 9 am."

questions.texte: Identify the future tense used in "are planning to build" and explain why it is used.
questions.reponse: Present Continuous (arranged plan) - they have already discussed it.

questions.texte: Identify the future tense used in "will provide" and explain why it is used.
questions.reponse: will (prediction/assurance) - it is a promise from the government.

questions.texte: Identify the future tense used in "are going to organise" and explain why it is used.
questions.reponse: going to (intention/plan) - they intend to do it.

questions.texte: Identify the future tense used in "will donate" and explain why it is used.
questions.reponse: will (prediction based on evidence) - based on the support they see.

questions.texte: Identify the future tense used in "is coming" and "am meeting" and explain why they are used.
